name: Paht Thai
recipeIngredient: 4 Slender green onions (sliced into 1 inch lengths)
recipeInstructions:
Paht Thai is a noodle dish almost everyone seems to like. A tangle of slender rice noodles is sauteed with garlic, shallots, and an orchestra of sweet, sour, and salty ingredients that play a piquant symphony of Thai flavors. A handful of fresh bean sprouts provides a cooling contrast to the hot, seasoned noodles, and circles of lime invite you to bring sourness to center stage as you begin to eat.
Traditional ingredients are salty dried shrimp; crispy pieces of fried, pressed bean curd; sweet-sour nuggets of pickled white radish; chopped peanuts; flat, green garlic chives; and a balanced chorus (sweet, sour, salty, hot) of palm sugar, tamarind, vinegar, lime, brown bean sauce, and crushed dried red chilies.
Thai cooks often tweak the classic formula to create signature versions. For a simple, delicious version:
1. Soak rice noodles in warm water to cover for 15-20 minutes. Meanwhile, prepare all remaining ingredients and place them next to the stove.
2. Heat oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat until very hot. Add garlic and toss until golden (about 30 seconds).
3. Add shrimp and cook until pink and opaque (no more than 1 minute). Remove from pan.
4. Return oil, then add softened noodles. Spread into thin layer, pull into clumps, and gently turn over several times as they soften.
5. Season with fish sauce and sugar, adding peanuts for a few more turns.
6. Add bean sprouts, green onions, and shrimp mixture. Cook until beansprouts are heated through (1 minute).
7. Transfer to serving platter and squeeze lime juice over top. Garnish with remaining beansprouts and lime wedges.
Serves 1 as main course, 2 as appetizer.
